key_insight: The paper extends stress calculations to localized-orbital DFT and shows that linear-scaling stress can converge well with modest density-matrix cutoffs.
engineering_relevance: This enables reliable stress-based simulations, including large-scale isothermal-isobaric molecular dynamics, in scalable DFT workflows.
method: Derived stress expressions for localized-basis DFT in both exact diagonalization and linear-scaling regimes
Implemented the approach in the large-scale Conquest DFT code
Benchmarked convergence of stress versus density-matrix range across materials with different band gaps
evidence_signal: The abstract reports convergence tests showing excellent stress accuracy at modest cutoffs and stable, accurate NPT molecular dynamics.
watch_item: Stress accuracy depends on the chosen density-matrix cutoff, so insufficient range may degrade convergence for some materials.
paper_title: Stress calculation in linear scaling DFT: convergence and dynamics
